title: B-NLS on Mcs: focusing bright soliton with matched u = N*phi_x
ic: N(x,0) = 2 * sech^2(sqrt(2)*(x+5)), phi(x,0) = 0.25*x (so phi_x = 0.25 everywhere), u(x,0) = 0.25 * N(x,0). [On Mcs: m = u - N*phi_x = 0.]
ground_truth: If the compound-soliton hypothesis is right, the soliton structure propagates approximately rigidly with the boost. Mass conserved exactly. m(x,t) should stay close to zero.
stress_question: Does Strang-splitting B-NLS (Madelung-Psi for NLS sector + RK4 or upwind for u equation) preserve Mcs? Does ||m(t)||_2 grow numerically? What is the natural method coupling between the two sub-systems?
parameters_to_sweep: Time-splitting strategy: Strang first vs Lie-first; coupling order; dt in {0.001, 0.0005}.
